sources.exact_document_title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
sources.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
sources.clinical_setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for urticaria definition and classification; detailed management text was not accessible and is not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Official abstract: definition and presentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

exact_sections.heading: Official abstract: impact and scope
exact_sections.evidence_summary: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

archive.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
archive.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
archive.setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Official abstract: definition and presentation
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Official abstract: impact and scope
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

sources.exact_document_title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
sources.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
sources.applicability_note: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.

exact_sections.heading: Key recommendations; 2.1 Definition of anaphylaxis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

exact_sections.heading: Key recommendations and trigger discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.
